howItWorks.intro: This stock profit calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Profit = (Sell Price - Buy Price) × Shares - Commissions
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How is stock profit calculated?
faq.answer: Profit = (Sell Price × Shares - Commission) - (Buy Price × Shares + Commission). This is your gross profit before taxes.

faq.question: What about taxes on stock profits?
faq.answer: Short-term gains (held under 1 year) are taxed as ordinary income. Long-term gains (over 1 year) get preferential rates of 0%, 15%, or 20%.

faq.question: What about dividends?
faq.answer: This calculator covers trading profit only. Dividends received during your holding period would add to your total return.

faq.question: Are commissions still a factor?
faq.answer: Most major brokers (Fidelity, Schwab, Robinhood) now offer $0 commission stock trades. Options and some other products may still have fees.

faq.question: Is the Stock Profit Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Stock Profit Calculator?
sections.body: The Stock Profit Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your buy price per share, number of shares, sell price per share, you get instant results including total cost, total revenue, profit/loss. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total cost right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your buy price per share and need to find the right total cost. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Buy Price per Share ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your buy price per share — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: number of shares, sell price per share, total commissions. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total cost, total revenue, profit/loss. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your buy price per share before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total cost and total revenue — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage your finances.
If you're unsure about your total commissions, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
